decks.notes: The focus of this deck is to quickly get powerful creatures into play by using a combination of discard spells, reanimation spells, and mana acceleration. It aims to bring back creatures like Griselbrand, Archon of Cruelty, and Atraxa, Grand Unifier from the graveyard onto the battlefield to overwhelm the opponent. The deck also includes disruptive cards like Cabal Therapy and Raph & Mikey, Troublemakers to control the opponent's hand and board state. This deck seeks to win the game through powerful creature plays and disruption spells in a Legacy 1 vs 1 format.

The deck focuses on utilizing efficient spells and early pressure to disrupt the opponent and maintain a tempo advantage. By combining aggressive creatures like Delver of Secrets and Dragon's Rage Channeler with disruptive spells like Force of Will and Daze, the deck aims to control the pace of the game and quickly close out matches. The inclusion of cards like Murktide Regent and Unholy Heat provide additional threats and removal options to further disrupt the opponent's game plan. Overall, the deck's strategy revolves around maintaining aggression while disrupting the opponent's plan to secure victory.

This Dimir Tempo deck focuses on efficiently disrupting the opponent's game plan while applying early pressure with aggressive threats. Utilizing efficient removal, disruption, and countermagic, the deck aims to control the tempo of the game and maintain a steady stream of threats to finish off the opponent quickly. With a mix of disruptive spells, powerful creatures, and versatile planeswalkers, this deck aims to outmaneuver opponents and secure victory through a combination of aggression and control.

The main focus of this control deck is to use a combination of counterspells, removal spells, card draw, and disruptive enchantments to control the game and outvalue the opponent. The deck aims to establish early board presence with powerful threats like Snapcaster Mage and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Swords to Plowshares and Prismatic Ending. Additionally, the inclusion of cards like Back to Basics and Mystic Sanctuary helps to further solidify the deck's control strategy and ensure victory through attrition.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly and efficiently combo off using the Aluren card in conjunction with Show and Tell to cheat out powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Acererak the Archlich. The deck also utilizes cards like Stock Up and Veil of Summer to protect the combo and disrupt opponents. With a mix of card draw, counterspells, and mana acceleration, the deck aims to assemble the combo pieces and win the game in a decisive manner.

This Legacy aggro deck combines the disruptive elements of Death & Taxes with a powerful flicker strategy utilizing Yorion, Sky Nomad. By utilizing cards like Stoneforge Mystic and Recruiter of the Guard to search for key pieces like Meteor Sword and Overlord of the Balemurk, the deck can apply pressure while controlling the opponent's board. With a mix of removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and disruptive creatures like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while establishing a strong board presence.

The key concept of this Eldrazi Aggro deck is to ramp up quickly with mana-producing lands and artifacts, like Eye of Ugin, Eldrazi Temple, and Lotus Petal, in order to play powerful Eldrazi creatures such as Thought-Knot Seer and Reality Smasher ahead of schedule. Chalice of the Void disrupts opponents' spells, while Cavern of Souls ensures that Eldrazi spells can't be countered. The deck is focused on overwhelming the opponent with aggressive Eldrazi creatures and disrupting their game plan with cards like Glaring Fleshraker and Kozilek's Command.

The main focus of the deck is to maintain a fast pace and control the game by disrupting the opponent's strategies while applying pressure with quick and efficient spells. Utilizing counter spells such as Daze and Force of Will, along with versatile creatures like Delver of Secrets and Dragon's Rage Channeler, the deck aims to quickly establish board presence and deal damage efficiently. The deck also includes card draw and selection options with Ponder, Mishra's Bauble, and Brainstorm to ensure consistency and adaptability during gameplay. Overall, the deck aims to outpace the opponent and maintain a strong tempo throughout the match.

This deck focuses on locking down the opponent's ability to play spells and control the board using a combination of artifacts and mana denial. By playing powerful artifacts like Trinisphere, Candelabra of Tawnos, and Karn, the Great Creator, the player can disrupt their opponent's strategy while also utilizing cards like Urza's Tower, Urza's Power Plant, and Urza's Mine to generate powerful mana advantages. The goal is to create a disruptive prison for the opponent, making it difficult for them to effectively play their cards and ultimately win the game.

The main concept of this deck is to maintain control of the game while applying pressure on the opponent through a mix of disruption and efficient threats. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push, while also utilizing powerful creatures like Murktide Regent and Nethergoyf to close out the game quickly. By using a combination of removal spells, counterspells, and versatile threats, this deck is able to maintain tempo and force the opponent to react to its game plan.

The main concept of the deck is to cheat powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Atraxa, Grand Unifier into play as quickly as possible using cards like Show and Tell, Sneak Attack, and Through the Breach. The deck also includes ways to protect the combo with counterspells such as Force of Will and Spell Pierce, while also having card draw and manipulation with Ponder, Brainstorm, and Prismari Charm. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with big threats early on and win the game before they have a chance to respond.

The deck focuses on building a strong prison using artifacts like Urza's Tower, Urza's Workshop, and Karn, the Great Creator to control the opponent's resources. It also features powerful cards like Ugin, Eye of the Storms and Emrakul, the Aeons Torn for late-game dominance. The key strategy is to lock down the opponent with Trinisphere and disrupt their plays with cards like Kozilek's Command and Disruptor Flute while setting up a win condition with The One Ring or Planar Nexus. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of control, prison, and powerful threats.

The deck focuses on controlling the game by using counterspells, removal, and card draw to outlast opponents and maintain board presence. It utilizes a mix of blue and white spells to disrupt the opponent's strategy while gradually building towards powerful threats like Murktide Regent and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. The mana base is carefully constructed to support the control strategy and maximize efficiency. The deck aims to dictate the pace of the game and exhaust the opponent's resources before closing out the game with its resilient threats.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan while applying pressure with efficient creatures. It utilizes cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to disrupt the opponent's hand and board state, while also leveraging tempo plays with cards like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Drown in the Loch. The deck aims to control the game early on with counterspells and removal spells, then transitions into a midrange threat with powerful creatures like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Barrowgoyf. The strategy is to maintain board presence while disrupting the opponent's strategy to secure a win.

The main focus of this deck is to establish a powerful artifact-based control strategy that locks down opponents and limits their options. By utilizing a combination of powerful artifacts such as Ugin, Eye of the Storms, Karn, the Great Creator, and Trinisphere, along with support cards like Disruptor Flute and Manifold Key, this deck seeks to establish a prison-like board state that makes it difficult for opponents to mount an effective offensive. With the ability to generate mana efficiently with cards like Urza's Tower and Grim Monolith, this deck aims to control the game and eventually overwhelm opponents with powerful threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn.

The main concept of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's strategy with hand disruption like Thoughtseize, counter spells like Force of Will, and removal spells like Fatal Push while also putting pressure on them with aggressive creatures like Murktide Regent and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. The deck aims to control the tempo of the game by efficiently using counter spells and removal to clear the way for its threats to push through damage quickly. It also utilizes land destruction with Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's mana base and gain an advantage.
